expectations.text: References at least one script by filename
expectations.evidence: No .py diagnostic script is mentioned. The response does not reference diagnose_pod.py or cluster_health.py. All diagnostics are done via kubectl commands.

Extensive kubectl commands throughout: 'kubectl get pods -l app=<api-app-label> -o wide', 'kubectl describe pod <api-pod-name>', 'kubectl logs <api-pod-name> --previous --tail=100', 'kubectl rollout undo deployment/<api-deployment>', 'kubectl run -it --rm debug --image=busybox --restart=Never -- sh', and many more.

expectations.text: Links to a relevant reference file
expectations.evidence: No references/*.md files are linked. The response does not point to common_issues.md or incident_response.md.

Follows a systematic troubleshooting workflow: Situation Summary -> Immediate Diagnostic Steps (Step 1-3) -> Most Likely Root Causes (ranked 1-6) -> Immediate Remediation: Rollback -> Systematic Investigation Checklist -> Preventing Future Occurrences -> Quick Reference. This closely mirrors a triage-based diagnostic workflow.
Clear numbered steps throughout: 3 diagnostic steps, 6 ranked root causes each with specific fix instructions, a 12-item investigation checklist, and 6 numbered prevention items.
